msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 123 is another attachment-only continuation. The required current-turn files are present, and I did not substitute any File Library material.

Turn 123 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 122:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓHwithπH∘Φ=πS,and the attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G⟶F=P⋊G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G isomorphism condition.

The frozen proof chain remains the uniform certificate chain:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness mechanism remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; a compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH.No structural case should be reopened under the present inputs. The non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower is closed by the induction Cm=Lm; the Ef-coefficient tower is closed after exact normalization of the local row to (2,t) and the Koszul–Witt cascade; and the finite filtered Nielsen compactness step globalizes the finite solutions.The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are only the prompt/problem/history set; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict gate.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 123 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 122. The theorem coordinate remainsΓS≅GΓH,via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN123-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
